paper.title: Characterizing State Space Model and Hybrid Language Model Performance with Long Context
paper.abstract: Emerging applications such as AR are driving demands for machine intelligence capable of processing continuous and/or long-context inputs on local devices. However, currently dominant models based on Transformer architecture suffers from the quadratic computational and memory overhead, which hinders applications required to process long contexts. This has spurred a paradigm shift towards new architectures like State Space Models (SSMs) and SSM-Transformer hybrid models, which provide near-linear

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Custom SSM kernels like selective scan dominate the inference runtime on edge platforms, accounting for over 55% of latency due to their sequential, element-wise nature.
technical_details: Custom SSM kernels like selective scan
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices, but the actual bottleneck may be the sequential, element-wise nature of custom SSM kernels like selective scan, which dominate the inference runtime on edge platforms.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
